channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Zuckerberg dines with Trump in Mar-a-Lago
item.description: Donald Trump dined on Wednesday with Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg at the president-elect’s Mar-a-Lago club in Florida.
item.dc:creator: Adriana Gomez Licon, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Americans' opinion of Republican Party on the rise
item.description: In the poll, 45 percent of Americans said they feel “favorable” toward the Republican Party, up 6 points from an Economist/YouGov poll in late October, when 39 percent said they felt favorable toward GOP.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: No Labels pushes back after opposition to third-party run
item.description: No Labels, a centrist group, claims in court documents that some Democrats worked to oppose a third-party candidate
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: Democrat Derek Tran defeats GOP Rep. Michelle Steel in Southern California swing House district
item.description: Democrat Derek Tran has defeated two-term Republican U.S. Rep. Michelle Steel in a Southern California House district that was specifically drawn to give Asian Americans a stronger voice on Capitol Hill.
item.dc:creator: Michael R. Blood, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Homan taking death threats against him 'more seriously' after Trump officials targeted with violent threats
item.description: Incoming Trump border czar Tom Homan says his family has received death threats in the past and that the recent threats against Trump officials means he now takes them more seriously.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ohio governor signs 'bathroom bill' into law, restricting students from using opposite-sex restrooms
item.description: Republican Ohio Gov. Mike DeWine has signed a bill into law that will prevent schools from allowing students of the opposite sex into restrooms and locker rooms.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump turns to outsider to shake up Navy, but his lack of military experience raises concerns
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump’s nominee to be secretary of the Navy, John Phelan, has not served in the military or had a civilian leadership role in the service.
item.dc:creator: Tara Copp, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: 3 Americans held for years in China have been released, the White House says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Three American citizens imprisoned for years by China have been released and are returning to the United States, the White House said Wednesday, announcing a rare diplomatic agreement with Beijing in the final months of the Biden administration.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, AAMER MADHANI and MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Meet the medical contrarians picked to lead health agencies under Trump and Kennedy
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump has assembled a team of medical contrarians and health care critics to fulfill an agenda aimed at remaking how the federal government oversees medicines, health programs and nutrition. On Tuesday night, Trump nominated Dr.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Here are the candidates most likely to fill JD Vance’s Senate seat
item.description: Many candidates are vying to replace Vice President-elect JD Vance in the Senate, and a new poll gives a glimpse at who the top candidates are.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russia expels two German journalists in tit-for-tat retaliation, ministry says
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — Russia's Foreign Ministry said Wednesday it has revoked the accreditation of two employees with the German ARD broadcaster and ordered them to leave the country in what it described as a retaliation to German authorities' move targeting two journalists from Russia's state TV.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump team says Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire deal brokered by Biden is actually Trump's win
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Biden administration kept President-elect Donald Trump's incoming administration closely apprised of its efforts to broker the ceasefire deal between Israel and Hezbollah that took effect early Wednesday, according to the outgoing Democratic administration.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Lawmakers call for progress on marijuana law before end of Biden administration
item.description: Progressive lawmakers are calling on President Biden to take additional steps on federal marijuana law before his term comes to an end in January. In a letter sent to Biden and Vice President Harris, Democratic Reps. Barbara Lee (Calif.), Earl Blumenauer (Ore.) and Ilhan Omar (Minn.) and Sens.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, NewsNation

item.title: End of Trump prosecutions renews scrutiny of DOJ's pace
item.description: Special counsel Jack Smith's move to dismiss Donald Trump's two federal indictments has sparked finger-pointing from those eager to see the president-elect held to account, with critics airing their frustrations at the Department of Justice (DOJ) and the courts for the anticlimactic end to the case.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: History of the Thanksgiving turkey pardon: When the White House began the long-held holiday tradition
item.description: The annual turkey pardoning is a long-held Thanksgiving tradition for the White House. But the history of exactly when the pardon began "gets tricky.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Reps McGovern, Massie urge Biden to pardon Julian Assange to 'send a clear message' on press freedom
item.description: U.S. Reps. James McGovern and Thomas Massie wrote a letter to President Biden urging him to pardon WikiLeaks founder Julian Assange to "send a clear message" on press freedom.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump names speechwriter Vince Haley as Domestic Policy Council chief
item.description: President-elect Trump announced on Tuesday night that Vince Haley, his campaign speechwriter, will be the next chief of the Domestic Policy Council (DPC). “As Director of the Domestic Policy Council, Vince will help make life better for ALL Americans, and unify our Country through SUCCESS.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Biden team briefed Trump transition on Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire
item.description: President-elect Trump's transition team has received briefings from the chief architect of the Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire deal under President Biden, a senior administration official said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump signs MOU with Biden White House for next phase of transition
item.description: After a lengthy delay, President-elect Trump has signed off on a memorandum of understanding (MOU) with the White House, which will allow officials to meet with counterparts at departments and agencies ahead of the January transition of power.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump won about 2.5M more votes this year than he did in 2020. This is where he did it
item.description: It’s a daunting reality for Democrats: Republican Donald Trump's support has grown broadly since he last sought the presidency. In his defeat of Democrat Kamala Harris, Trump won a bigger percentage of the vote in each one of the 50 states, and Washington, D.C., than he did four years ago.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT, MAYA SWEEDLER, PARKER KAUFMANN and HUMERA LODHI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel, Lebanon's Hezbollah agree to a ceasefire to end nearly 14 months of fighting
item.description: Israel approved a United States-brokered ceasefire agreement with Lebanon's Hezbollah on Tuesday, setting the stage for an end to nearly 14 months of fighting linked to the ongoing war in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Lauren Boebert Cameo page disappears amid House ethics concerns
item.description: Rep. Lauren Boebert (R-Colo.) briefly offered videos on Cameo, a website on which public figures sell personalized videos to fans, before the page went offline amid questions about whether it would have violated House ethics rules.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's hush money case in limbo after judge adjourns sentencing
item.description: New York Judge Juan Merchan faces a choice of freezing President-elect Trump's hush money case in place until he leaves the White House or tossing it entirely, underscoring how Trump's election win has changed the dynamic of his legal travails.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Biden proposes Medicare and Medicaid cover costly weight-loss drugs for millions of obese Americans
item.description: Millions of Americans with obesity would be eligible to have popular weight-loss drugs like Wegovy or Ozempic.
item.dc:creator: AMANDA SEITZ, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump aide sought payment to 'promote' potential picks: Reports
item.description: Trump's attorneys are investigating allegations that one of his top aides offered promotion in exchange for money, according to reports.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Manhattan top federal prosecutor Damian Williams to resign in December
item.description: A top federal prosecutor in Manhattan is set to resign in December, the U.S. attorney’s office for the Southern District of New York said in a Monday press release.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota DNR delays decision on key proposed copper-nickel mine permit
item.description: The Minnesota Department of Natural Resources has delayed a decision until late next year on a key permit for the proposed NewRange Copper Nickel project in northeastern Minnesota, the mine formerly known as PolyMet. The company called the agency’s decision “unfortunate.
item.dc:creator: Dan Kraker, MPR News

item.title: Special counsel moves to dismiss election interference case against President-elect Donald Trump
item.description: Special counsel Jack Smith asked a federal judge to dismiss the case accusing President-elect Donald Trump of plotting to overturn the 2020 election.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Rays must decide fate of stadium deal by Dec. 1, Pinellas commission chair says
item.description: The Pinellas County Commission is seeking answers from the Tampa Bay Rays organization on its embattled stadium deal.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Jessica Tisch to become 2nd woman to lead NYPD
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Sanitation Commissioner Jessica Tisch became the top cop in New York City on Monday. She's the second woman to ever be the NYPD commissioner.
item.dc:creator: Kala Rama, PIX 11

item.title: In deep blue Minneapolis, many Somali voters withheld support for Harris
item.description: According to state elections data, support for Vice President Kamala Harris in parts of Minneapolis with concentrations of East African immigrants and Somali voters was weaker than in the city as a whole.
item.dc:creator: Cari Spencer, MPR News

item.title: State GOP, Dems react to Trump selecting Pam Bondi to be next AG
item.description: Former Florida Attorney General and Temple Terrace native Pam Bondi has been selected to be President-elect Donald Trump’s next U.S. Attorney General.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC
